sections.paragraph:
πανδοχεῖον, ου, τό (πανδοχεύς ‘innkeeper’; Strabo 5, 3, 9; 12, 8, 17; Epict., Ench. 11; Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. 4, 39 p. 157, 28; TestJud 12:1; PSI 99, 3; PEg2, 35=ASyn. 42, 31.—The older form πανδοκεῖον in Aristoph., Ran. 550, but also in Theophr., Char. 11, 2; Polyb. 2, 15, 5; Epict. 2, 23, 36; 4, 5, 15; Appian, Mithrid. 20, §76; Plut., Crass. 556 [22, 4]; Palaeph. 45; Aelian, VH 14, 14; Polyaenus 4, 2, 3; ins [Nachmanson 81]; Phrynichus rejects the form with χ, p. 307 Lob.; cp. B-D-F §33; W-S. §5, 27e; Mlt-H. 108. Though lacking in Philo [except in a Fgm., s. JRoyse, NovT 23, ’81, 13f] and Joseph., the word was taken over by Jews as a loanw. [Billerb. II 183; Dalman, Gramm.2 187] and has survived to the pres. day in Arabic) inn, where a traveler may find a night’s lodging Lk 10:34.—Pauly-W. 36, 3, ’49, 520–29. M-M.

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Palaeph Palaeph , date uncertain—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Nachmanson Nachmanson = EN., Laute und Formen der Magnetischen Inschriften—Lists 3, 6

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

NovT NovT = Novum Testamentum (periodical)—List 6

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

Billerb. Billerb. = HLStrack and PBillerbeck, Kommentar z. NT aus Talmud u. Midrasch—List 6

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

pres. pres. = present

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 754
